title: Intense Italian Vegetables Frittata
description: A savory, cheesy, and satisfying frittata perfect for a midnight craving, featuring raw vegetables and a crispy, cheesy crust. Great for a shojin-friendly meal option.
instructions: In a large skillet, heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil over medium heat. Add the diced onion and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the diced zucchinis, bell peppers, and tomatoes to the skillet, and cook until the vegetables are tender, about 10-12 minutes, stirring occasionally.
In a large bowl, whisk together the eggs, Parmesan cheese, and a pinch of salt and pepper. Set aside.
Add the cooked vegetable mixture to the bowl with the egg mixture, and stir to combine.
Pour the egg and vegetable mixture into a greased 9-inch (23 cm) round baking dish.
Bake the frittata in the preheated oven for 25-30 minutes, or until the edges are golden brown and the center is set.
Remove the frittata from the oven, and sprinkle the mozzarella cheese on top.
Return the frittata to the oven and bake for an additional 2-3 minutes, until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Remove the frittata from the oven, and let it cool for 5 minutes before slicing and serving.
Serve warm, sliced into wedges or squares.
Enjoy your Intense Italian Vegetables Frittata!
